lines.text: Central Perk, Frank and Alice are there talking with Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! I can't believe my little brother is married!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Oh I know!! (Both he and Alice squeal hysterically)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You guys, why didn't you tell me you were eloping?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: 'Cause it just sorta happened, y'know we were at the courthouse, we were having lunch...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait, wait, why were you at the courthouse?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: We were having lunch. Yeah and then all of the sudden we were like, "Hey! Y'know, we're here, having lunch let's get married!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, a year and a half ago I didn't even know I had a brother, and now I have a sister too. (They all hug, and Frank and Alice start kissing.) Okay. Okay. Stop it, don't. So, I gotta get you a gift now. Is there anything you need?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Uhh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: We've been trying to get pregnant, uh pretty much ever since we got engaged, we thought we'd get a jump on things, y'know no one's getting any younger.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: See the thing is umm, we're not able to y'know, uh, conceive.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: And we've tried everything, we've seen a bunch of doctors.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Yeah, and they-and they say that our-that our only chance to have a baby is that if they take my sperm, her egg and put it together in a dish and then put it into another girl. So we were wondering if you could be the girl that we could put it into.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (shocked) That's a really nice gift. I was thinking of like a gravy boat.
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Monica, Rachel, and Chandler are trying to throw cards into a vase.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering with Ross) Hey! You guys! Check it out, check it out! (He's wearing a blue blazer) Guess which job I got.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know, but Donald Trump wants his blue blazer black.
The gang is stunned.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Blue blazer back. He-he wants it back.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: But you-you said black. Why would he want his blue blazer black?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you-you know what I meant.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, you messed it up. You're stupid.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So what job did you get Joe?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, ah, tour guide at the museum. Yeah, Ross got it for me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, how can you be a tour guide, don't you have to be a dinosaur expert or something?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, not really. They give you all the information, it's uh, it's like memorizing a script. (Making like a tour guide) "And on your left, you have Tyrannosaurus Rex, a carnivore from the Jurassic period.

lines.speaker: Chandler, Monica, and Rachel
lines.text: Great!! That's great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh actually Joey, it's the Cretasous period.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah but, I can pronounce Jurassic.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (entering) Hey!!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Guess what. Frank Jr., and Alice got married!

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh my God!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And! And, they're gonna have a baby! (The gang is shocked.) And! And, they want me to grow it for them in my uterus. (The gang is stunned into silence.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're really thinking about having sex with your brother?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ewww! And "Oh no!" It's-they just want me to be the surrogate. It's her-it's her egg and her sperm, and I'm-I'm just the oven, it's totally their bun.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What did you tell them?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, they said that I had to think about it first, but what is there to think about? I'm gonna be giving them the greatest gift you can possibly give.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're gonna be carrying their baby and give them a Sony Play Station?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, this really is an incredible thing to do for them, but there are things to think about.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, like you're gonna be pregnant. I mean pregnant.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pheebs, you're talking about putting your body through an awful lot, I mean morning sickness, uhh, labour, and it's all for somebody else!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, what's your point?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, the stuff I just mentioned.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wow! I don't know if I could ever do that. I always figured the first time I had a baby was with somebody I love and that baby would be a...keeper.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know you guys were a lot more supportive when I wanted to make denim furniture.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, Pheebs, listen, if you decide to do this, we'll be supportive like crazy.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We just want you to think it through.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, honey, maybe you can talk to somebody who's had a baby. Like your mom?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My mom never gave birth. Oh! But my birth mom did.
Central Perk, Chandler and Kathy are kissing.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Umm, (moves her hand's down to his butt) I love this touchy. Can I take it to work with me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yeah, sure, it's not mine anyway. It can with the pants.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Oh! (They kiss and she leaves)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I am so jealous.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You guys are really right...there aren't you?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes. Right where?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: In the beginning where y'know it's all sex and talking and sex and talking and...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, you-you gotta love the talking.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And the sex?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, we haven't had sex yet. Okay, what's the big deal? Y'know? This is special, and I want our love to grow until we move on to the next level.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Chandler that is so nice.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That is really nice...lying! No way is that the reason!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why? Just because you're not mature enough to understand something like that?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, he's right, I'm totally lying.
Ross makes an "I was right, and you weren't face." And Rachel does Ross's little I'm-flicking-you-off-but-I'm-not-giving-you-the-finger banging of the fists.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Then what is it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, Kathy's last boyfriend was Joey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And you're afraid you won't be able to...fill his shoes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I'm afraid I won't be able to make love as well as him.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I was going for the metaphor.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, and I was saying the actual words.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So big deal, so Joey's had a lot of girlfriends, it doesn't mean he's great in bed.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We share a wall! So either he's great in bed, or she just likes to agree with him a lot.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Sweetie, with you it's gonna be different. The sex is gonna be great, 'cause you-you guys are in love.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just go for it Chandler.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, you should.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, you should, really.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Go on.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, all right, I'll go sleep with my girlfriend. But I'm just doing it for you guys.
The museum, Joey is giving a tour to a bunch of school kids.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, now the Mastodon is from the semi-late Jurassic period.

lines.speaker: Smart Kid
lines.text: Isn't the Mastodon from the Pliocene Epic?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Shhh! This is a museum, no talking. Right down here, (Motions to a fossilised dinosaur foot.) we have a large foot. (Sees Ross working in one of the display cases.) Uhh, and over here we have Ross Geller. (Knocks on the glass) Everyone wave 'Hi' to Ross. Ross is one of our most important scientists, look at him, hard at work. (Ross does the old "Putting a cigarette in your ear and pulling it out of your mouth trick.") Okay, moving right along. Come on.
Phoebe Sr.'s house, there's a knock on the door.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: It's open! Come in!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'm late.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Oh, that's okay, it gave me time to finish glazing my nipples.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! You really go all out when you're expecting company.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: No, I was working on my pottery.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Ooh! Oh, I didn't know that you did...pot.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Well, yeah, mostly nudes. It combines my two passions, pottery and erotica.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, erotiery!

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Hey! Okay! Well thanks for coming out to see me. I just-I just thought it would be a very good idea to talk about this baby stuff in person. Y'know...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: I really don't think it's a very good idea, Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Well, because you'd be giving up a baby, and I-I really don't-I don't know if there's anything I can say that could make you understand the pain of giving up a baby. So, umm, (Picks up a puppy in the box next to the couch.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no! No-no! I understand the pain! Don't-don't hurt the puppy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: No-no-no, the-the puppy's yours.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I get a puppy!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Well, yeah! I mean yeah, but only for three days.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: I realise I don't have any right to start get all parenty on you and everything now, but umm, (Sees that Phoebe isn't paying attention and is busy mimicking the puppy.) uhh... Phoebe, would you please look at me and not the puppy, it's very important.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: I mean, I know what I'm talking about. I gave up two babies, and I only wish I had someone there that had given up babies, that could tell me how terrible it is to give up babies. I just think that, it would be something you will regret every single day for the rest of your life. So, how ever hard it is to give up this puppy, it would be like a million times harder to give up a child. (Phoebe is playing with the puppy again, and not listening) I really shouldn't have given you the puppy first.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, I'm sorry.
The museum's worker cafeteria, Joey is eating lunch with the rest of the tour guides. Another tour guide tries to sit down in a seat Joey saved for Ross.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uhh, do you mind sitting there. I'm-I'm saving this for my friend Ross.

lines.speaker: Tour Guide
lines.text: You mean Dr. Geller?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Doctor? Wow! I didn't know he had a nickname.

lines.speaker: Tour Guide
lines.text: Oh, he won't sit here. Only the people in the white coats sit over there, (Points to there table) and only the people in the blue blazers sit here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, how-how come?

lines.speaker: Tour Guide
lines.text: That's just the way it is.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's crazy.

lines.speaker: Tour Guide
lines.text: Maybe it's crazy in a perfect world, a world without lab coats and blazers, but you not in a perfect world, you in a museum now. See that scientist in the classes, he and I used to play together all the time in grade school, but now...(Turns around) Peter! Hey, Peter! It's me Rhonda! From PS-129! I shared my puddin' which you man! I gave you my Snack Pack! (to Joey) See, he pretend he don't even here me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I-I think everybody's pretending they don't hear you. Anyway, look, I don't know about you and your jackets and your separate tables, but Ross is one of my best friends, and if I save him a seat, I'm telling you, he will sit in it! (Ross enters and goes over to the white table) Ross! Ross! Over here, man! I-I saved you seat.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's okay, I'm cool over here. I'll catch up with you later, Joey. (Joey is shocked.)
Another woman enters without a coat or blazer and tries to sit at the 'blue' table.

lines.speaker: Tour Guide
lines.text: Op, this is saved. (Joey wonders why) Gift shop.
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, Joey is eating dinner, Monica and Rachel are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey, uh, I'm really, really sorry about what happened in the cafeteria today.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's no big deal. Hey, y'know, you do what you gotta do. Right?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But hey, it's not just me, I mean the scientists and the tour guides never sit together.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whatever.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's like that everywhere, Joey! Okay, Mon, back me up here. Where you work the uh, waiters eat with the waiters, right? And the chefs eat with the other chefs, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I eat by myself in the alley because everybody hates me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, Ross, really it's-it's no big deal. Y'know you wear a white coat, I wear a blue blazer, if that means we can't be friends at work, then so be it. Y'know, hey I understand. Y'know? Hey, when I'm in a play and you're in the audience, I don't talk to you, right? So it's y'know, it's uh, it's cool. I'll see you tomorrow. (Leaves)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, when we're in the audience he doesn't talk to us, but he does wave.
Monica and Rachel's, the next morning, Phoebe is there with her puppy and is trying to sing it to sleep as Chandler enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why is Phoebe singing to Carl Mulden?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, y'know what, I think it's time for puppy to go out again. Come on, let's go to the balcony.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Umm, the street. Come on, let's go to the street. Ooh, listen, don't go onto the balcony until after I get back. (Leaves)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to Chandler) So, did you do it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, yes, we had the sex.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-oh, it was bad?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It was fine, y'know? But she didn't agree with me as strongly as she agreed with Joey. She was more like, "I see you point, I'm all right with it."

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, it was the first time. Y'know, there's not always a lot of agreement the first time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, not girls anyway, guys agree (snaps her fingers) like that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, you have to help me! Okay? I mean, I know what to do with a woman, y'know, I know where everything goes, it's always nice. But I need to know what makes it go from nice to, "My God! Somebody's killing her in there!"

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I'm gonna show you something a lot of guys don't know. Rach, give me that pad, please? (She does so and Monica starts drawing on it) All right. Now...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, you don't have to draw an actual wo-whoa! She's hot!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now everybody knows the basic erogenous zones. (She starts labelling them) You got one, two three, four (Chandler is shocked to find out there's more than three), five, six, and seven!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (shocked) There are seven?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Let me see that. (Monica shows her) Oh, yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Points to one) That's one?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's kind of an important one!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, y'know-y'know what, I was looking at it upside down.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, y'know, sometimes that helps. (She realises what that could've meant.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (continuing) Now, most guys will hit uh, 1-2-3 and then go to 7 and set up camp.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That-that's bad?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well if you go to Disneyland, you don't spend the whole day on the Materhorn.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well you might if it were anything like 7!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right uh, the important thing is to take your time, you want to hit 'em all, and you mix 'em up. You gotta keep them on their toes.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oo, toes!! Well, for some people. (Chandler eyes her and her toes.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right. Umm, you could uh start out with a little 1, a 2, a 1-2-3, 3, 5, a 4, a 3-2, 2, a 2-4-6, 2-4-6, 4, (Rachel starts getting worked up) 2, 2, 4-7, 5-7, 6-7, 7, ...7.....7...7-7-7-7-7-7-7-7-7-7-7-7...(mouths 7)! (They both lean back on the couch satisfied.)
The museum cafeteria, Joey is eating with the tour guides as Ross enters.

lines.speaker: Older Scientist
lines.text: Dr. Geller, there's a seat over here. (Motions to an empty chair at the 'white' table.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you, Dr. Phillips, but I'm having my lunch at this table, here in the middle. I'm having lunch right here, with my good friend Joey, if he'll sit with me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (standing up) I will sit with you Dr. Geller. (He goes over to his table and they shake hands.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Y'know, we work in a museum of natural history, and yet there is something unnatural about the way we eat lunch. Now, I look around this cafeteria, and y'know what I see, I see-I see division. Division, between people in white coats and people in blue blazers, and I ask myself, "My God why?!" Now, I say we shed these-these coats that separate us, and we get to know the people underneath. (He takes off his coat and throws it down.) I'm Ross! I'm divorced, and I have a kid!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (stands up, and throws his coat on the floor) I'm Joey! I'm an actor! I don't know squat about dinosaurs!

lines.speaker: Another Tour Guide
lines.text: (standing up and removing his coat) I'm Ted, and I just moved here a month ago, and New York really scares me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, there you go!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, you hang in there Teddy!

lines.speaker: Older Scientist
lines.text: I'm Andrew, and I didn't pay for this pear.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, good-good for you.

lines.speaker: Tour Guide
lines.text: I'm Rhonda, (motions to her breasts) and these aren't real! (Joey and Ross look at each other, shocked)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow, Rhonda.

lines.speaker: Another Scientist
lines.text: I'm Scott.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, okay, Scott!

lines.speaker: Another Scientist
lines.text: And I need to flip the light switch on and off 17 times before I leave a room or my family will die.
Central Perk, Phoebe, with the puppy, Monica, and Rachel are sitting on the couch.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: My mom's gonna be here any minute. I can't do this, I can't give him up. Yes-no, I can. I don't want to. But I can. No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oo, I can't watch this, it's like Sophie's Choice.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know, I never saw that.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, it was only okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, I can't do this. My mom was right. If I can't-if I can't give him up, then there's no way I can give up a baby. Ohh, God, Frank and Alice are gonna be so crushed. What-what else, what else can I give 'em-a kidney!

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: (entering with Frank) Hi!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: Uhh, we were just in the neighbourhood, so...

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Yeah, so we just thought we'd stop by and let you know there's still no pressure.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: None. But if there was something you wanted to tell us, we're just gonna be right over there (points to the counter) having coffee.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: (noticing the puppy) Oh, who's this little guy?! (Grabs the puppy)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! Ooh! Umm!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Oh, he's so cute, he reminds me of my old dog, Tumour.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: You are so precious, I could just take you home.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, why don't you?

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Are you serious?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uh-huh, yeah!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Oh, thanks.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you doing?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, I'm really okay with this. Y'know why? 'Cause look at them, and I made that, so... I know it's gonna be like a million times harder to give up a baby but, oh my God, it's gonna feel like a million times better, right? I wanna do this. (To Frank and Alice) I wanna carry your baby.

lines.speaker: Alice
lines.text: (shocked) Oh! Oh! Oh! Thank you so much! You don't know what this means to us! Oh!

lines.speaker: Frank
lines.text: Oh my God, I think I'm gonna cry!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's gonna be so great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: (entering) Hi! What's going on?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I-I gave them the puppy and it made them so happy that I decided I'm gonna carry their baby.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: But Phoebe...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No-no-no, I know, but you and I are different people though, and this is a totally different situation, and I know that I am not gonna regret this.

lines.speaker: Phoebe Sr
lines.text: Oh, I-I-I understand all that, but it's just-that was my puppy.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!
Closing Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Monica and Rachel are sitting at the table.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Would you ever be a surrogate for anyone?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: It depends on who asked.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What if I asked?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Mon, sure.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes. (Pause) You're not asking are you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! Totally!
Kathy runs in, hair all out of place, and hugs Monica.

lines.speaker: Kathy
lines.text: Oh! Thank you! Thank you! Thank you! Yes! Thank you! (Runs back to Chandler)
End
